description: Lucerne at dawn, Jungfraujoch at noon, Zurich at dusk. Switzerland doesn't offer views — it offers vertical poetry. Premium rail passes, lakeside hotels, and chocolate fondue evenings included.
Neuschwanstein Castle rising from the Bavarian mist, the Rhine Valley's half-timbered villages, Berlin's electric energy. Germany covers every mood — history, nature, and unforgettable evenings.

destination: Italy — La Dolce Vita in Every Piazza
packageName: Italy — La Dolce Vita in Every Piazza
description: Rome's Colosseum, Venice's canals, Tuscany's cypress-lined roads, and Amalfi's cliffside lemons. Italy is not a country — it's a sensory overload, served with pasta and a Campari sunset.
The Eiffel Tower at midnight, the lavender fields of Provence, Mont Saint-Michel at high tide. France gives you art, cuisine, and the feeling that you're living inside a painting.

destination: Sweden & Finland — Chasing the Northern Lights
packageName: Sweden & Finland — Chasing the Northern Lights
description: Aurora borealis over Lapland's frozen lakes, husky sledding at minus 20°C, glass igloos under an electric sky. Sweden and Finland offer the most extraordinary nights on earth — literally.

The Isle of Skye's black Cuillins, Eilean Donan Castle rising from a loch, single malts swirling in ancient distilleries. Scotland is the most dramatic landscape in Europe that most travellers haven't properly seen.
Rijksmuseum Rembrandts, Amsterdam's canal ring at golden hour, Bruges' medieval chocolate-box streets, Ghent's alleyways. The Low Countries pack remarkable density of beauty into very few kilometres.

Santorini's blue domes at sunset, Mykonos' windmills, the Acropolis at dusk. Greece is the birthplace of beauty — and it shows in every sunset, every feta-crumbled salad, and every whitewashed alleyway.

destination: Spain — Flamenco, Gaudí & the Sun-Drenched South
packageName: Spain — Flamenco, Gaudí & the Sun-Drenched South
description: Barcelona's Sagrada Família, Madrid's Prado, the Alhambra's Moorish grandeur, and Ibiza's legendary sunsets. Spain contains multitudes — and rewards those who stay long enough to feel them.

destination: Eastern Europe Highlights — Prague, Budapest & Vienna
packageName: Eastern Europe Highlights — Prague, Budapest & Vienna
description: Three grand imperial capitals in one extraordinary circuit: Prague's Charles Bridge at dawn, Budapest's thermal baths, Vienna's Sachertorte in a fin-de-siècle café. Elegance without the Western price tag.

destination: Russia — Moscow, St. Petersburg & the Trans-Siberian
packageName: Russia — Moscow, St. Petersburg & the Trans-Siberian
description: St. Basil's Cathedral in Red Square, the Hermitage's 3 million masterworks, the Catherine Palace's golden halls, and the world's most dramatic train journey threading Siberia's vast silence.
Cherry blossoms along the Philosopher's Path, bullet trains threading rice paddies, ramen at 2am in Tokyo, Kyoto's 10,000 torii gates. Japan operates on another level of excellence — in everything.

The Great Wall at Mutianyu stretching to the horizon, Shanghai's Pudong skyline reflected in Huangpu River, Xi'an's Terracotta Army standing in silent formation for 2,200 years. China's scale is humbling.

Seoul's Bukchon Hanok village at golden hour, hiking Seoraksan's granite peaks, K-drama film locations, night markets overflowing with tteokbokki. South Korea is compact, intense, and deeply satisfying.

destination: Singapore — The City That Does Everything Right
packageName: Singapore — The City That Does Everything Right
description: Gardens by the Bay's Supertrees glowing at night, hawker centres where a Michelin-starred meal costs SGD 6, Sentosa's beaches, and the cleanest, safest, most efficient city on earth.

A kingdom that measures its wealth in Gross National Happiness. Tiger's Nest monastery clinging to a cliff, prayer flags snapping in thin air, yaks grazing below Himalayan peaks. Bhutan is the most serene place you will ever visit.

destination: Thailand — The Land of Smiles (and Temples)
packageName: Thailand — The Land of Smiles (and Temples)
description: Bangkok's golden spires at dawn, Chiang Mai's lantern festival, Krabi's limestone karsts rising from jade water. Thailand delivers the perfect ratio of culture, beach, food, and value that no other destination quite matches.

destination: Vietnam — A Country, Not Just a Destination
packageName: Vietnam — A Country, Not Just a Destination
description: Halong Bay's limestone towers piercing morning fog, Hoi An's lantern-lit ancient town, Hanoi's street food chaos at its finest. Vietnam moves from north to south like a perfectly constructed novel.

destination: Sri Lanka — The Teardrop of the Indian Ocean
packageName: Sri Lanka — The Teardrop of the Indian Ocean
description: Sigiriya's rock fortress, tea-estate trains threading misty Ella hills, colonial Galle Fort, blue whales off Mirissa, wild leopards in Yala. Sri Lanka is astonishingly varied for an island you can drive across in a day.

destination: Bali — The Island of the Gods
packageName: Bali — The Island of the Gods
description: Ubud's rice terraces cascading to the valley, Tanah Lot at sunset, Seminyak's world-class beach clubs, and spiritual healing energy that permeates every temple and ceremony. Bali is transcendent for couples.

destination: India — Kerala, Rajasthan & the Golden Triangle
packageName: India — Kerala, Rajasthan & the Golden Triangle
description: The Taj Mahal at sunrise, Jaipur's Pink City bazaars, Kerala's houseboat sunsets on silent backwaters. India is not one destination — it's thirty. We build the best ones into one seamless journey.

destination: Uzbekistan — Samarkand, Bukhara & the Blue Domes
packageName: Uzbekistan — Samarkand, Bukhara & the Blue Domes
description: Registan Square's three medrasa facades glittering with Timurid tilework, Bukhara's mud-brick labyrinth, Shah-i-Zinda's mosaic canyon. Uzbekistan is the Silk Road's most spectacular surviving act — and MGT's most under-the-radar bestseller.

destination: Azerbaijan — Where Europe Meets the Orient
packageName: Azerbaijan — Where Europe Meets the Orient
description: Baku's Old City walls rising beside flame-shaped glass towers, Sheki's caravanserai, Gobustan's mud volcanoes, and the Caspian's mercurial light. Azerbaijan is the surprise of the decade.

destination: Turkey — Istanbul, Cappadocia & the Turquoise Coast
packageName: Turkey — Istanbul, Cappadocia & the Turquoise Coast
description: Hot air balloons over Cappadocia's fairy chimneys at sunrise, the Blue Mosque's six minarets at dusk, gulets sailing the Aegean past sunken Lycian ruins. Turkey delivers luxury and antiquity in the same breath.

destination: Jordan — Petra, Wadi Rum & the Dead Sea
packageName: Jordan — Petra, Wadi Rum & the Dead Sea
description: The Siq opening to reveal Petra's Treasury, Wadi Rum's red sand desert glowing at dusk, floating effortlessly in the Dead Sea's mineral-dense silence. Jordan is a short flight and a world apart.

Muscat's white-washed Royal Opera House, the Grand Canyon-like Jebel Akhdar gorge, Nizwa Fort's Friday goat market, and Wahiba Sands' golden dunes at first light. Oman is what you imagined Arabia to be.

A private water villa where the floor is glass and the Indian Ocean is your carpet. Manta ray snorkel at dawn, sunset dolphin cruise, bioluminescent beach walk at midnight. The Maldives is not a holiday — it's a religion.

destination: Salalah, Oman — Arabia's Green Season Secret
packageName: Salalah, Oman — Arabia's Green Season Secret
description: During the Khareef (monsoon) season, arid Dhofar transforms — waterfalls flow, frankincense trees drip with resin, coconut palms bend in misty sea winds. Salalah in summer is unlike anything else in Arabia.

destination: Seychelles — The Granite Garden of Eden
packageName: Seychelles — The Granite Garden of Eden
description: Anse Source d'Argent's sculpted granite boulders, Vallée de Mai's prehistoric coco de mer palms, and sea turtles nesting on Aldabra. Seychelles was designed by a world that hadn't yet decided to be practical.

Champagne-pink sunsets, turquoise lagoons rimmed by coral reef, undersea waterfalls viewed from above, and resorts that understand what luxury actually means. Mauritius is one of the most complete island experiences on earth.

destination: Phuket & Thai Islands — The Beach You Dreamed Of
packageName: Phuket & Thai Islands — The Beach You Dreamed Of
description: Phi Phi Islands' vertical limestone walls, Khao Sok's jungle-lake kayaking, Koh Lanta's long-tail boats at golden hour. Thailand's islands are achingly beautiful — and still genuinely affordable.

destination: Bali & Lombok — Island Twins, Different Souls
packageName: Bali & Lombok — Island Twins, Different Souls
description: Bali's temples, rice terraces, and world-class spa culture paired with Lombok's raw, unhurried Gili Islands — white sand, no motorbikes, and sunsets that make you stay one more night, then another.

destination: USA — East Coast, West Coast & Vegas
packageName: USA — East Coast, West Coast & Vegas
description: New York City's skyline at midnight, the Grand Canyon's mile-deep silence, Las Vegas' neon fever dream, and the Pacific Coast Highway unspooling along California's cliffs. America is the world's most cinematic road trip.

destination: Canada — Niagara, Rockies & the Great North
packageName: Canada — Niagara, Rockies & the Great North
description: Niagara Falls' roar, Banff's impossibly turquoise Lake Louise, Vancouver's mountain-sea horizon, and the Northern Lights dancing over Yukon. Canada is the USA's quieter, more polite, and arguably more beautiful cousin.

destination: Mexico — Riviera Maya, Mexico City & Cenotes
packageName: Mexico — Riviera Maya, Mexico City & Cenotes
description: Chichen Itza's pyramid casting its serpent shadow at equinox, Tulum's Mayan cliff temples above turquoise Caribbean, cenote swimming in prehistoric sinkholes. Mexico is deeper and more extraordinary than its resort reputation suggests.

destination: Peru — Machu Picchu, the Sacred Valley & the Amazon
packageName: Peru — Machu Picchu, the Sacred Valley & the Amazon
description: The Inca citadel emerging from cloud forest at 2,430 metres, the Sacred Valley's terraced ruins, Lake Titicaca's floating islands, and the Amazon's cathedral of green. Peru is the most rewarding country on earth to understand slowly.

Iguazu Falls' 275 cascades straddling two countries, Rio's Christ the Redeemer surveying the Carnival city, and Patagonia's granite Torres del Paine rising above glacial ice. South America's southern cone at its absolute wildest.

destination: Kenya & Tanzania Safari — The Great Migration
packageName: Kenya & Tanzania Safari — The Great Migration
description: 1.5 million wildebeest crossing the Mara River in a scene unchanged for 10,000 years, lions on acacia silhouettes at dusk, and lodge breakfasts while elephants graze 50 metres away. The African safari is the world's most extraordinary wildlife spectacle.

Stone Town's labyrinthine Arab alleys, clove and cardamom drifting from doorways, dhow sunsets over the Indian Ocean, and Nungwi's white sand reef just steps from your beach bungalow. Zanzibar pairs perfectly with a mainland Tanzania safari.

destination: South Africa — Cape Town, Safari & the Garden Route
packageName: South Africa — Cape Town, Safari & the Garden Route
description: Table Mountain's tablecloth cloud at sunrise, the Garden Route's fynbos-scented coast, a Kruger Park leopard in a tamboti tree, and Franschhoek's Michelin-calibre wine estates. South Africa is the most complete country in the world.

The Great Pyramid at Giza — the only surviving ancient wonder — Luxor's Valley of the Kings, Abu Simbel's colossi carved into Nubian sandstone, and a Nile felucca drifting past temples that predate Rome by 2,000 years.

Fez's medieval medina unchanged since the 9th century, Marrakech's Djemaa el-Fna at midnight, the Atlas Mountains' Berber villages, and sleeping in a desert camp as the Erg Chebbi dunes turn gold at dawn.

Cozumel's coral walls, Jamaica's Dunn's River Falls, the private island paradise of CocoCay — Royal Caribbean's Western Caribbean circuit is the definitive introduction to island-hopping at sea. Aboard the Harmony of the Seas, with waterslides, a surf simulator, and 20 restaurants.

St. Thomas's duty-free paradise, Nassau's pink colonial buildings, Puerto Rico's El Morro fortress, and Norwegian's Freestyle Cruising ethos — dine when you want, do what you want, no fixed schedules.

Fourteen nights covering Barbados, Martinique, St. Kitts, Antigua, Tortola, and St. Maarten. MSC's European elegance meets Caribbean colour — black-tie nights in the Grand Ballroom, beach afternoons in turquoise coves.

destination: Celebrity Cruises — Southern Caribbean & ABC Islands
packageName: Celebrity Cruises — Southern Caribbean & ABC Islands
description: Aruba's white sand and constant sunshine, Bonaire's world-class diving, Curaçao's Dutch-Caribbean architecture and Handelskade's candy-coloured waterfront. Celebrity's premium Modern Luxury experience elevates every port.

destination: Disney Cruise Line — Bahamas & Disney's Private Island
packageName: Disney Cruise Line — Bahamas & Disney's Private Island
description: Castaway Cay — Disney's private Bahamian island — is the crown jewel: a 1,000-foot stretch of white sand, waterslides, and adults-only beach clubs. Pair with Nassau's colonial heart and pirate-themed evenings onboard.

Barcelona's Gaudí skyline, Marseille's Calanques, Genoa's carruggi alleyways, Naples' pizza and Pompeii, and Rome's Colosseum on a day excursion from Civitavecchia. Seven nights that feel like a semester in European civilisation.

Palma de Mallorca's cathedral rising from the sea wall, Valencia's City of Arts, Toulon's azure bay, Monte Carlo's harbour of superyachts. Costa brings Italian flair to the Riviera's most glamorous coastline.

From Venice's Grand Canal to Santorini's clifftop villages, Athens' Acropolis, Rhodes' walled city, and Dubrovnik's Game of Thrones walls — Royal Caribbean's extended Med circuit covers the greatest hits without rushing any of them.

Mykonos' windmills and Little Venice, Ephesus' marble-paved Roman avenue, Istanbul's Blue Mosque at sunset, Kusadasi's bazaar carpets. Celebrity's Retreat deck makes every port arrival a private moment of arrival.

destination: Silversea — Aegean & Holy Land Luxury Voyage
packageName: Silversea — Aegean & Holy Land Luxury Voyage
description: From Athens to Jerusalem — Santorini's caldera, the caves of Malta, Crusader Acre, Jerusalem's Old City, the Dead Sea — aboard an ultra-luxury all-suite ship where every port comes with a dedicated butler briefing.

A boutique French expedition yacht threading the Dalmatian coast's 1,200 islands — Hvar's lavender fields, Kotor Bay's fjord-like beauty, Dubrovnik's pearl, and Montenegro's wild Lovćen National Park — for 200 passengers only.

destination: Viking River — Grand European Danube Tour
packageName: Viking River — Grand European Danube Tour
description: Fifteen days from Amsterdam to Budapest — the Rhine's Lorelei cliffs, Rüdesheim's wine village, Regensburg's perfectly intact medieval old town, Vienna's Ringstrasse, and Budapest straddling the Danube like a lit crown jewel. Viking's longships redefine what a river can be.

Basel's Art Basel district, the Rhine Gorge's 19 castles in 65 km, Rüdesheim's Drosselgasse, Cologne's twin-spired cathedral, and Amsterdam's canal ring. The Rhine valley is the most scenic river in Europe — Avalon's floor-to-ceiling windows prove it.

Keukenhof's 7 million tulips in bloom, Amsterdam's canal district, Kinderdijk's 19 windmills reflected in still water. The Tulip Time cruise runs only in April when the Netherlands becomes one vast floating garden — book months ahead.

destination: Scenic — Douro Valley, Portugal: Wine & Soul
packageName: Scenic — Douro Valley, Portugal: Wine & Soul
description: Porto's Ribeira waterfront, Douro's terraced quintas producing Port wine since the 17th century, Salamanca's golden sandstone plazas, and Régua's wine museum. The Douro is Portugal's most beautiful secret — a river that tastes like a fortified wine.

Nuremberg's Christkindlesmarkt glowing in December, Vienna's Rathausmarkt, Cologne's Cathedral Christmas Market, Regensburg's medieval Thurn und Taxis market. The Rhine in December is the most atmospheric Christmas anywhere on earth.

Avignon's Papal Palace, Arles' Roman arena where gladiators fought, Pont du Gard's 2,000-year-old Roman aqueduct, and Châteauneuf-du-Pape's vineyards. The Rhône through Provence is Impressionism made navigable.

Singapore's supertrees, Kuala Lumpur's Petronas Towers, Penang's street food alleyways, Phuket's limestone bays, Ho Chi Minh City's motorbike rivers, and Ha Long Bay's mythical karst formations. Southeast Asia in twelve extraordinary days.

Sakura season at sea — Yokohama's harbour cherry blossoms, Kyoto's Philosopher's Path exploding pink, Hiroshima's Peace Park, and Kagoshima's Sakurajima volcano smoking across the bay. Japan in bloom is Japan at its most devastatingly beautiful.

destination: Cunard — Asia Grand Voyage: India to Japan
packageName: Cunard — Asia Grand Voyage: India to Japan
description: Mumbai's Gateway of India, Sri Lanka's Galle Fort, Singapore's colonial Raffles, Vietnam's imperial Hue, and Kobe's harbourfront. Cunard's Queen Victoria carries white-gloved afternoon tea through Asia's most extraordinary coastlines.

Sydney Harbour Bridge's steel arch at dawn, Milford Sound's waterfalls plunging into a fjord, Queenstown's adrenaline capital, Fiordland's raw wilderness, and the Great Barrier Reef's coral palette. The Australia-New Zealand circuit is the Southern Hemisphere's finest cruise.

destination: Norwegian — South Pacific Islands: Bora Bora & Fiji
packageName: Norwegian — South Pacific Islands: Bora Bora & Fiji
description: Bora Bora's overwater bungalows, Moorea's shark-feeding lagoon, the raw jungle of Samoa, and Fiji's kava ceremonies. The South Pacific is the most remote major cruise destination — and the most visually breathtaking.

Longyearbyen at 78° North — polar bears outnumber humans — glaciers the size of cities, walrus colonies on ice floes, and the midnight sun burning gold at 3am. Hurtigruten's ice-class expedition ships carry scientists, guides, and guests into the high Arctic with equal seriousness.

Bergen's Bryggen wharf, Flåm's mountain railway, Geiranger fjord's double cascade waterfalls, and the Northern Lights aurora swirling above Tromsø. Viking's Bergen–Bergen circuit is the definitive fjord cruise, refined to a precise science.

Ilulissat Icefjord's cathedral icebergs (UNESCO World Heritage), Disco Bay's midnight sun, Reykjavik's geothermal city, the Blue Lagoon's milky-silica waters, and the Golden Circle — two of the most dramatic geological landscapes on earth, back to back.

Glacier Bay's 16 tidewater glaciers calving with sonic booms, Ketchikan's totems, Juneau's whale-watch tenders, Skagway's Klondike Gold Rush history, and Tracy Arm Fjord's mirror-surfaced walls of blue ice. Alaska from the sea is Alaska as it should be seen.

The Drake Passage's roaring swells, the South Shetland Islands' penguin rookeries, the Antarctic Peninsula's iceberg cathedrals, zodiac landings on the seventh continent, and the most profound silence any human can experience — aboard an ultra-luxury all-suite expedition ship.

The most famous ship in the world on the most ambitious itinerary on earth — 115 nights, 52 ports, 6 continents. White-gloved dinners in the Britannia Restaurant, lectures in the Royal Court Theatre, and a transatlantic crossing as crossing should be made — aboard the last ocean liner ever built.

destination: Regent Seven Seas — All-Inclusive Luxury Mediterranean
packageName: Regent Seven Seas — All-Inclusive Luxury Mediterranean
description: Every suite, every shore excursion, every drink, every speciality restaurant, every spa treatment, every flight — included. Regent's true all-inclusive formula removes every friction point from luxury travel. The Mediterranean aboard Seven Seas Explorer is as good as cruising gets.

The most intimate luxury ships on the Pacific — 600 guests, all ocean-view suites, complimentary caviar and champagne on the bow, and a crew that remembers your name, your drink, and your preference for the early or late sunset spot. Tahiti to Tokyo via Fiji, Vanuatu, PNG, and Japan.

destination: MSC — Arabian Gulf & Red Sea from Dubai
packageName: MSC — Arabian Gulf & Red Sea from Dubai
description: The only major cruise that departs Dubai directly — Muscat's white-washed port, Khasab's Musandam fjords by dhow, Aqaba's gateway to Petra, Safaga for Luxor's temples, and the Suez Canal's engineering marvel. No flights required.

destination: Costa — Dubai to India & Sri Lanka
packageName: Costa — Dubai to India & Sri Lanka
description: Departing Dubai for Mumbai's Gateway of India, Goa's Portuguese-tiled churches, Cochin's Chinese fishing nets, and Colombo's Dutch Fort — then back to Dubai. A subcontinental circuit from your home port, no transatlantic positioning required.

A 2-hour sail through Dubai Marina's canyon of glass towers aboard a traditional wooden dhow, refurbished into a floating restaurant. International buffet dinner, live Tanoura dance, and skyline views from Ain Dubai to the Marina Yacht Club.

The original dhow route — sailing the historic Creek between Deira's spice souqs and Bur Dubai's wind-tower architecture. The contrast between old Arabia and the Burj Khalifa skyline, all from one wooden deck.

destination: Private Dhow Charter — Family & Group Celebration
packageName: Private Dhow Charter — Family & Group Celebration
description: Charter the entire dhow for your group — birthdays, anniversaries, corporate gatherings. Customisable menu, decorations, and music, with the whole vessel reserved exclusively for your party along the Marina or Creek.

A sleek catamaran with a sundeck and shaded lounge — gliding past Ain Dubai, Bluewaters Island, and the Marina towers. Faster and more stable than a dhow, with panoramic 360° views and onboard refreshments.

Watch the Marina towers turn gold as the sun sets, with a live BBQ grill on deck, unlimited soft drinks, and a DJ setting the mood. A more relaxed, social alternative to the formal dinner buffet cruise.

A 30–45 minute adrenaline-laced speedboat run past Burj Al Arab, Atlantis, and the Marina skyline — for those who want the views without the dinner-cruise pace. Photos and video included on most packages.
The perfect entry point into Dubai yachting — a sleek 42-footer for couples, small families, or close friends. Cruise past Ain Dubai and Palm Jumeirah with your own private captain and crew.
Comfortably hosts 19–30 guests — the sweet spot for birthdays, team offsites, and family celebrations that actually feel like a party. Sun deck, lounge seating, and optional BBQ or catering add-ons.

destination: 71–88ft Yacht — Floating Estate for Corporate Events
packageName: 71–88ft Yacht — Floating Estate for Corporate Events
description: Expanded lounges, larger sun decks, and richer finishes — built to host 30–65 guests for serious corporate hospitality, product launches, or milestone celebrations on the water.

Multi-deck superyachts with cinema-grade sound, VIP lounges, and full chef setups — the kind of private floating venue reserved for milestone corporate galas, weddings, or VIP entertaining at the highest level.
A premium sedan with a professional, uniformed chauffeur — discreet, punctual, and built for business travel, hotel transfers, or simply arriving in quiet comfort. RTA-licensed and fully insured.

The classic stretch limousine — leather salon interior, mood lighting, and a spacious cabin built for bridal parties, anniversaries, and milestone celebrations across Dubai's most photogenic backdrops.
An elevated SUV-class limousine seating up to 10 — privacy partitions, premium audio, and the legroom your group actually needs. Ideal for families, small corporate groups, or VIP guest transport.

destination: Buses, Cars & Vans Across the UAE
packageName: Buses, Cars & Vans Across the UAE
description: From a single executive sedan to a 66-seater corporate coach — reliable, RTA-compliant ground transport connecting every emirate, every airport, every itinerary.
A chauffeur with a name board waiting inside the terminal, luggage assistance, and a direct ride to your hotel in a premium sedan or SUV. The first impression of your UAE holiday, handled properly.
